resources.string: Set the service address, test the connection, and choose a mode.
Choose the kind of address the Android app should use to reach your relay.
Receive a shared link and send it to the forwarding service.
Open Settings to enter the forwarding service address.
Tip: Android Emulator usually uses http://10.0.2.2:8080 to reach your computer.
Use your Tailscale, ZeroTier, or other private relay address when the phone and computer are on different networks.
Use your Tailscale, ZeroTier, or other private relay address, and save the same relay auth token in Settings before testing from another network.
You can change this address in Settings.
Test the connection in Settings first, then choose a mode.
You can change the default mode in Settings.
Only the latest 20 tasks are kept.
Share an article here and recent tasks will appear.
The task will be sent with the current forwarding rule.
Missing task ID. The status cannot be shown.
This page keeps syncing the latest task status from the forwarding service.
Use the service base URL, for example http://192.168.1.12:8080 .
Use http://10.0.2.2:8080 when the app runs in the Android Emulator and relay runs on your computer.
Use your computer\'s LAN IP, such as http://192.168.1.12:8080, when the phone and computer are on the same network.
Use your Tailscale or ZeroTier address, such as http://100.101.102.103:8080, when the phone and computer are on different networks.
Paste the forwarding service token if remote access requires it
Optional on local networks. Leave this blank if your forwarding service does not require a token.
A forwarding service auth token is already saved on this phone. Keep it in sync with the relay AUTH_TOKEN value.
Private-network relay access usually requires the same AUTH_TOKEN that your forwarding service is using. Paste it here before testing the connection.
This private-network relay will use the saved forwarding service token. Test the connection again after changing either the address or token.
0.0.0.0 is only a relay bind address. Enter a real emulator, LAN, or private-network address here instead.
localhost and 127.0.0.1 only work on the same device. For Android use 10.0.2.2, a LAN IP, or a private-network address.
This still looks like a LAN address. For different networks, switch to a Tailscale, ZeroTier, or other private relay address.
This looks like a private-network address. That is fine as long as your private network app is connected.
Test the connection first, then choose a default mode.
Loading configuration from the forwarding service...
Test the connection first to fetch the mode list.
The forwarding service address is invalid. Use an http or https URL.
0.0.0.0 is only for the relay bind address. Enter a real relay address instead.
localhost and 127.0.0.1 only work on the same device. Use 10.0.2.2 for the emulator, or your computer\'s LAN/private relay address on a phone.
Failed to fetch the mode list.
Connection is ready and the mode list has been loaded.
Choose the language used in the app. Tap Save to apply it.
No http(s) URL was found in the shared text.
This shared text does not contain a supported http(s) link.
A valid article link is ready to send.
Waiting for the forwarding service response.
Waiting to send to the forwarding service.
The phone is sending the prepared link.
The forwarding service did not respond. The app will retry.
The phone has handed the task to the forwarding service.
The task did not reach the forwarding service.
Manual verification is required in the managed browser.
WeChat returned a verification page. Open the article in the OpenClaw managed browser, complete the verification, and then submit the task again.
Complete verification in the managed browser
Open this WeChat article in the OpenClaw managed browser profile and finish the verification step. Then come back and submit the task again.
The earlier WeChat verification may have expired.
The managed browser profile may have lost the earlier WeChat verification or login state. Open the article again in the OpenClaw managed browser and complete verification one more time.
Verify the managed browser profile again
Open this article again in the OpenClaw managed browser profile and complete verification or login again. Then submit the task again.
This WeChat link opened a parameter error page. Share the original article link again.
WeChat returned a parameter error page instead of the article body. Go back to the article and share the original link again.
A cancel request has been sent to the forwarding service.
Failed to send the cancel request.
The task has already completed. No cancel request is needed.
The task has already failed and cannot be cancelled.
The current OpenClaw session is busy. The forwarding service will wait and retry.
The current OpenClaw agent session is being used by another run. Wait a moment, or avoid using the same agent at the same time.
The forwarding service could not reach the model provider. Try again later.
OpenClaw hit a network error while calling the model provider. Check the current network or proxy settings and try again later.
Task received and waiting to start.
Processing command exited with an error
Open the article in the managed browser and complete verification.
Submit the task again after verification succeeds.
Open the article again in the managed browser profile.
Finish verification or sign in again, then retry the task.
Go back to the original article and share the original link again.
Avoid shortened or expired share pages when resubmitting.
Open the article once in the managed browser and make sure it loads fully.
Retry after the article body is visible.
Wait for the current run to finish and let relay retry automatically.
Avoid starting multiple tasks against the same target at the same time.
Check the current network or proxy settings on this machine.
Retry after the network path is stable again.
Check the local tool login or API key configuration.
Retry once to confirm whether the timeout was temporary.
Increase the timeout only if this task type is expected to run longer.
Check the configured command path and local tool installation.
Use diagnostics or relay doctor to confirm the environment.
Set the correct command path in Settings.
Switch to the mock or shell-command processing method for smoke tests.
Open Settings and add a trusted command template.
Switch to another processing method if you do not plan to use a custom command.
Open the task files and inspect stdout.txt and stderr.txt.
Fix the command error, then retry the task.
Review the result summary and task files for the reported reason.
Retry only after confirming the cause is temporary or corrected.
Review the result summary or task files if you need the full output.
Reuse the same settings if you want to submit another task.
Open the task details if you need to confirm where the task stopped.
Submit the task again when you are ready to continue.
Wait for the relay to finish the current step, or cancel the task if it is no longer needed.
Open the task files or diagnostic summary for more detail.
Retry only after checking the likely cause.
No explicit papers found. %1$d possible papers detected.
